message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/messages.py
note: My task (idle-timeout/tick) only touches streaming_listener.py per the ticket. If your feature (is_last_chunk) adds a field to StreamResponse in messages.py, I'll add a defensive getattr/hasattr-based set of is_last_chunk in my tick() code so it works whether or not your field exists yet, without editing messages.py myself. Let me know if you need to touch streaming_listener.py or streamify.py too - happy to adjust.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: I'm implementing StreamListener debug/trace logging (opt-in debug flag, guarded DEBUG logs) entirely within streaming_listener.py. Let me know your feature files so we can confirm no overlap.

type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: We both need this same file - it can't be split disjointly since our changes are both small, localized edits to the same class. Proposal: serialize instead of parallel-editing. I'll go first: add idle_timeout_s param, self._last_chunk_ts tracking, and a new tick() method (touches __init__ and receive(), pure additions, no logic changes to existing lines). I'll send DONE as soon as it's committed to the file. Then you layer your debug/trace logging on top of the updated file. If you'd rather go first instead, say so now and I'll wait for your DONE before touching the file. Either order is fine, just don't edit concurrently with me.

type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Correction: my feature is debug/trace logging only (a debug flag + guarded logging.DEBUG calls at start-detection/rolling-end-check/emit-chunk/flush/reset points inside receive()/flush()/__init__). It does NOT add an is_last_chunk field to StreamResponse or touch messages.py - the is_last_chunk in my log line is just a local bool I compute (e.g. self.stream_end) for the log message text, not a schema change. So we do have real overlap: both features touch streaming_listener.py. Proposal: I'll go first since my change is additive logging only (no new methods/params beyond debug/debug_logger kwargs), touching __init__, receive(), and flush(). I'll send DONE when finished (should be quick). Then you layer your idle-timeout/tick() work on top - your new method/state won't conflict with my log lines since I'm not restructuring control flow. Sound OK? If you'd rather go first, let me know and I'll wait.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Agreed - you go first (idle_timeout_s/_last_chunk_ts/tick(), additive to __init__ and receive()). I'll wait for your DONE, then layer my debug/trace logging (debug flag, debug_logger, guarded DEBUG logs at start-detection/rolling-end-check/emit-chunk/flush/reset) on top without touching your new lines. Will not edit the file until I see your DONE.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Agreed - you go first with the debug/trace logging (additive only, no control-flow changes), send DONE when committed to the file. I'll then layer idle_timeout_s/_last_chunk_ts/tick() on top without touching your log lines. I will not edit the file until I receive your DONE. Also noted: is_last_chunk is not part of your feature and doesn't exist on StreamResponse yet - I'll guard my code to only set it if the field exists on the dataclass, so no messages.py changes needed from either of us.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: I see conflicting signals - your REVISE said 'I'll go first' but your latest ACCEPT said 'you go first'. Checked git status/diff: file is untouched by either of us so far. To avoid a race, let's be explicit: I (agent2) will start editing streaming_listener.py NOW for the debug/trace logging feature, and will send DONE the moment it's committed. Please hold off touching the file until you see my DONE. If you were already mid-edit when you sent that last message, reply IMMEDIATELY with REVISE and I will stand down and wait for your DONE instead.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Our last two messages crossed - confirming final order per your latest message: I'll go first now (idle_timeout_s/_last_chunk_ts/tick(), additive only). Sending DONE shortly; you layer debug logging on top after that.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Let's stop the pronoun confusion. Final proposal, using explicit names: AGENT1 goes first (idle_timeout_s/_last_chunk_ts/tick()). AGENT1 sends a DONE message when committed. Only after AGENT2 (me) receives that DONE message will AGENT2 start editing the file for debug/trace logging. Reply ACCEPT to confirm this exact order (agent1 first, agent2 second) and then begin your edit - I will wait idle until your DONE arrives.

type: DONE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Finished: added idle_timeout_s param + _last_chunk_ts tracking in __init__/receive(), and a new tick(now_ts=None) method placed between receive() and flush(). All additive - no existing lines changed except adding a timestamp refresh (2 lines) inside receive() and a _last_chunk_ts=None reset in the existing allow_reuse reset branch. Tests pass (tests/streaming/ 15 passed). File is yours now - go ahead and layer your debug logging on top.

type: DONE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Finished debug/trace logging: added debug/debug_logger params to __init__, self._logger, and guarded DEBUG-level logs at start detection, rolling end check, emit chunk, flush, and state reset - all matching the exact required message formats. Only additive/guarded code (if self.debug and self._logger.isEnabledFor(logging.DEBUG): ...), no control-flow changes to existing branches beyond adding these log calls. Renamed the flush() branch return values to a shared  var so I could log once before returning, and split value_start_index slicing into a  var (used for the pre-lstrip preview) before assigning chunk_message - both are local-variable-only changes, no behavior change. Tests pass (tests/streaming/ 15 passed, 3 skipped). File is yours now for idle_timeout_s/tick().

Only additive/guarded code, no control-flow changes to existing branches beyond adding these log calls (plus two purely-local renames inside flush() and the start-detection branch to capture values for logging before further processing). Tests pass (tests/streaming/: 15 passed, 3 skipped). File is yours now for idle_timeout_s/tick() work. (Note: my previous DONE message got mangled by shell backtick expansion - this one supersedes it.)
